input.prompt: A .NET solution build has failed. The binary log from the failed build is available at build.binlog in the current directory. You do NOT have access to the original source files — only the binlog. Analyze this binlog file to diagnose all build failures, identify which errors are root causes versus cascading failures from dependent project failures, and suggest targeted fixes for each root cause.

input.initialPrompt: A .NET solution build has failed. The binary log from the failed build is available at build.binlog in the current directory. You do NOT have access to the original source files — only the binlog. Analyze this binlog file to diagnose all build failures, identify which errors are root causes versus cascading failures from dependent project failures, and suggest targeted fixes for each root cause.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Check dotnet availability and binlog file size

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Check dotnet availability and binlog file size

arguments.command: dotnet tool list -g 2>/dev/null; dotnet tool list --local 2>/dev/null

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to analyze a .NET build binary log (binlog) file to diagnose build failures. Let me start by examining the binlog file and extracting error information from it.

Binary log files (.binlog) are MSBuild binary logs. I can use the `dotnet build` tool or the `MSBuildStructuredLog` tool to analyze them. Let me first check what tools are available and then try to extract information from the binlog.

I'll need to:
1. Install the `dotnet-msbuild-log` tool or use `MSBuildBinaryLogReplayEventSource` to read the binlog
2. 
Extract errors and warnings from the log, then distinguish between root causes and cascading failures. Let me check if dotnet is available and use the appropriate tools to parse the binlog.

arguments.description: Check dotnet availability and binlog file size

arguments.command: dotnet tool list -g 2>/dev/null; dotnet tool list --local 2>/dev/null

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "which dotnet && dotnet --version 2>/dev/null; ls -lh build.binlog", "description": "Check dotnet availability and binlog file size", "initial_wait": 10}
